mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a single month, it signals an abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers risk-off positioning, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in housing starts and a decline in real personal savings rates indicate a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate revenue growth expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window, or SPX 20-day returns are uncorrelated with the combined proxy z-score.

mechanism_narrative: In a sustained industrial production expansion, rising capacity utilization eventually triggers supply bottlenecks that elevate input costs and compress profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive when INDPRO exceeds the threshold for three consecutive months, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during post-2008 recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential quarter-over-quarter increase in real GDP growth accelerates corporate earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the immediate 5-trading-day window as institutional flows adjust to stronger macroeconomic momentum.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between GDPC1 revisions and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.10, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero across all quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 2.5%, the cost of capital structurally increases, discounting long-duration equity cash flows and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following the rate threshold breach, or the signal's information coefficient remains below 0.02 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where employment growth slows while the unemployment rate remains sticky above 5.0% signals a weakening labor market without immediate recession confirmation, prompting equity de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS momentum and UNRATE level fails to explain more than 2% of the variance in 20-day SPX returns, or the signal produces false breakdowns during structural labor force participation shifts.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated consumer price inflation above 2.5%, real disposable income erosion reduces retail spending power, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below chance levels during supply-shock inflation episodes.
